$comment: WUNIAN project data schema — used as reference for HTML authoring, not as a data file consumed by the site (the site is pure static HTML). This schema documents what information every project page encodes.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier. Used as the HTML filename and image folder name.

title.description: Display title as shown in h1 and the index list. No hyphens. No em dashes. Title case or sentence case.

year.description: Year of project completion or primary work. Used for the data-year attribute in the index list and the year filter.

category.description: Appears in .project-label above the title. Typically 'Projects' or a more specific descriptor.

meta.description: Secondary line below the title (.project-meta). Combines context and year. No hyphens. Use em dash (—) as the separator between location/studio and year.

context.description: Structured breakdown of the meta field.

type.description: academic = Columbia MSAAD; professional = Lea Architecture; personal = independent; competition = open call

institution.description: For academic work: school name. Omitted from site display.

description.description:
Array of paragraph strings. Each becomes a

tag in the HTML. 2–4 paragraphs typical. No hyphens. No em dashes.
examples: As flooding becomes more frequent due to climate change in New York, having facilities that utilize this excessive amount of water and contribute to building a new culture could bring nuance to how the environment changes.
The Gowanus Water Center contains a water filtration system that integrates into the city's waterworks and a bathhouse portion for human wellness.

sections.description: Optional named sections within the project page, creating h2 headings that divide the image sequence into thematic groups.

heading.description: Section heading text. No hyphens. Appears as <h2> in the page.

description.description: Optional paragraph text for this section, placed after the h2 and before images.

images.description: Image entries belonging to this section.
All images in the project, in display order. Each entry corresponds to one .img-block in the HTML.

navigation.description: For text/reading pages only — prev/next links in a series.

label.description: Display label including number prefix, e.g. 'I. Movement and Duration'

tags.description: Internal taxonomy for the project. Not displayed on site. Used for future filtering or related-work logic.

published.description: Whether the project is currently linked from an index page. Pages can exist as files without being listed.

filename.description: Image filename relative to image/[slug]/. Always two-digit zero-padded JPEG.

alt.description: Alt text for accessibility. Describes the image content specifically — not the project name.
examples: Interior axonometric — public area A with reception desk and workstation cluster
Site analysis: heat and flooding overlay on Gowanus neighborhood

caption.description: Visible caption below the image. Optional. Format: 'Label: description. Additional note.' No hyphens. No em dashes.
examples: Public A: reception and dispatch. Workstations, filing storage, and a private office alcove.
Site map: main complex on Gowanus Canal with satellite bathhouses distributed through NYCHA superblocks.

source.description: Not displayed on site. Records the original source file for archival reference.

displayConstraint.description: CSS max-width override applied to this image's .img-block img, if not full container width.

examples.title: Viva Natatio
description: As flooding becomes more frequent due to climate change in New York, having facilities that utilize this excessive amount of water and contribute to building a new culture could bring nuance to how the environment changes. An ecological shift can be read as an opportunity for a change in lifestyle rather than a crisis.

The Gowanus Water Center contains a water filtration system that integrates into the city's waterworks and a bathhouse portion for human wellness. As water enters the project, rainwater is collected by water tanks, then enters the filtration system inside the facility.

images.alt: Site analysis showing heat and flooding overlay on Gowanus neighborhood
images.caption: Site analysis: heat and flooding conditions in Gowanus. Pink: higher temperature zones. Blue: projected flood extent.

images.alt: Entry courtyard with water tanks, bamboo, and terraced bathhouse volume
